prompt: hey can you enrich our record for Brightpath Consulting? they're a Tier 1 account but the data is embarassingly stale -- last touched maybe 14 months ago. Main contact is David Hargreaves, he was Head of Ops when we last spoke but I think hes moved. Also pretty sure their headcount jumped after that Series B last year. Their rep is Jess Moreno and they're in an active nurture sequence right now. Oh and Jess manually entered the direct phone number herself from a conference badge so dont mess with that. Need this cleaned up before our QBR Thursday.
expectations: To prevent wasted outreach to a stale contact, the output flags David Hargreaves for potential role change (no LinkedIn updates in >12 months) rather than assuming he is still Head of Ops at Brightpath Consulting -- a wrong-title email tanks response rates

To protect revenue on an active deal, the output does NOT overwrite the manually-entered direct phone number -- it explicitly flags it as a human-entered field and defers to Jess Moreno as record owner before any change, since reps trust CRM data they entered themselves

To protect the active nurture sequence from breaking, the output does NOT remove David Hargreaves from the sequence due to any email change -- instead it specifies pausing the sequence and alerting Jess Moreno to verify new contact details first, preventing a live prospect from going dark

To enable accurate segmentation and lead routing after the Series B, account-level enrichment addresses Brightpath Consulting headcount with a flag if the change exceeds 20%, plus the Series B funding captured as a news event -- these fields drive tier classification and ICP scoring

To ensure the QBR uses reliable data, the output includes a DATA QUALITY SCORE as a percentage of enriched records now complete, plus a RECORDS WITH MISSING MANDATORY FIELDS section listing any fields still unfilled -- giving the rep a clear picture of what is verified vs. what still needs manual work

To drive accurate pipeline forecasting, the output covers all six contact-level mandatory fields (full name, current title, current company, work email, direct phone, LinkedIn URL) and all five account-level mandatory fields (legal name, revenue estimate, headcount, headquarters, website) -- incomplete records create routing errors and missed handoffs
